title: What is Sayana Press?
content: Sayana Press is a contraceptive injection used to prevent pregnancy. It contains depot medroxyprogesterone acetate (DMPA) and is administered as a subcutaneous injection.

title: How does Sayana Press work?
content: Sayana Press prevents pregnancy by preventing ovulation, thickening cervical mucus to block sperm, and thinning the lining of the uterus.

title: How effective is Sayana Press?
content: When used correctly and every 13 weeks, Sayana Press is more than 99% effective in preventing pregnancy.

title: How often is Sayana Press administered?
content: One injection is required every 13 weeks, which is approximately every three months.

title: Is Sayana Press the same as Depo-Provera?
content: Both contain DMPA, but Sayana Press uses a smaller needle, is injected under the skin, and can be self-administered after proper training.

title: Injection sites
content: Sayana Press can be injected into the thigh, abdomen, or upper arm.

title: Self-injection
content: Many women can safely self-inject Sayana Press after receiving proper training from a healthcare professional.

title: Storage
content: Store Sayana Press at room temperature, away from direct sunlight, and out of the reach of children.

title: Who can use Sayana Press?
content: Most women can use Sayana Press, including women who have children, women seeking birth spacing, breastfeeding mothers after six weeks postpartum, and eligible adolescents.

title: Who should not use Sayana Press?
content: Women with unexplained vaginal bleeding, breast cancer, severe liver disease, or allergy to DMPA should consult a healthcare provider before using Sayana Press.

title: Can breastfeeding mothers use Sayana Press?
content: Yes. Sayana Press is generally considered safe for breastfeeding mothers after six weeks postpartum.

title: Common side effects
content: Common side effects include irregular menstrual bleeding, amenorrhea, weight gain, headache, mood changes, breast tenderness, dizziness, fatigue, mild abdominal discomfort, and reduced sexual desire.

title: Can Sayana Press cause infertility?
content: No. Fertility usually returns after discontinuing Sayana Press, although it may take several months.

title: What should I do if I miss my injection?
content: Visit a healthcare facility as soon as possible. If less than four weeks late, you may usually continue with your injection. If significantly late, pregnancy may need to be ruled out before another injection.

title: Does Sayana Press protect against STIs?
content: No. Sayana Press prevents pregnancy but does not protect against HIV, gonorrhea, chlamydia, syphilis, or other sexually transmitted infections. Condom use is recommended.

title: Does Sayana Press cause permanent infertility?
content: No. Fertility usually returns after stopping Sayana Press. There is no scientific evidence that it causes permanent infertility.

title: Emergency warning signs
content: Seek immediate medical attention if you experience heavy uncontrolled bleeding, severe chest pain, difficulty breathing, severe headache with vision changes, severe abdominal pain, high fever, signs of allergic reaction, or fainting.

title: Why are reminder systems important?
content: Reminder systems help users remember injection dates, track side effects, access educational information, and receive follow-up support.
